title: OpenAI eyes 2027 IPO — Anthropic now expected to list first
subtitle: Bloomberg reports OpenAI has pushed its public listing timeline from this fall into 2027 as tech-stock volatility weighs on the offering, leaving Anthropic — which has been pointing at October — on track to become the first frontier-AI IPO.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: OpenAI is now targeting an initial public offering as early as 2027, according to a June 26 Bloomberg report citing people familiar with the matter. Both OpenAI and Anthropic filed S-1 paperwork confidentially with the SEC earlier this month — OpenAI on June 9 at an $852 billion valuation, Anthropic on June 1 — but OpenAI leadership now expects Anthropic to reach the public market first. Anthropic has been pointing internally at an October listing window. OpenAI had been targeting a fall listing of its own before the slip.

Two things changed the math. First, technology-stock volatility through May and June has made underwriters more cautious about pricing the largest IPO in U.S. history into a soft window. Second, the U.S. export-control action against Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 — and OpenAI's own June 26 trusted-partner restriction on GPT-5.6 — have introduced an unpriceable regulatory variable that public-market investors will demand language for in the prospectus. Pushing into 2027 buys time for both the volatility and the policy fog to clear.

If Anthropic does list first, it would be the first frontier-AI IPO and would set the comp set for everything that follows — pricing, governance language around safety commitments, disclosures around government access programs, and the structure of dual-class equity tied to the long-term benefit trust. OpenAI's eventual filing will be compared against whatever Anthropic puts on the table. That sequencing is unusual: the larger, older, better-known company is now likely to be the follower, not the bellwether.

A takeaway for learners: read prospectuses. The first frontier-AI IPO will force into public language things that have been carefully vague in private — what counts as 'safe enough,' how much revenue concentration sits with a single customer (Microsoft for OpenAI, Amazon for Anthropic), how export-control exposure is disclosed, and what the company contractually owes the U.S. government. Whatever Anthropic's S-1 ends up saying becomes the template the industry argues with for years.

sources.title: OpenAI Considers 2027 IPO After Anthropic's Expected Public Debut — Bloomberg
